title: $itemnr
text: $itemnr Return the list item that corresponds to a given index number in a list. $itemnr ( N , List ) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description N Numeric Sequence number. List String Uniface list from which to extract an item. Return Values If the N th item is not found in List , the $itemnr function returns NULL and $procerror is set to -1129 . Table 2. Values commonly returned in $procerror after $itemnr Value Error Constant Meaning -1129 <UPROCERR_ITEM> Item with the requested index is not found. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The $itemnr function acts in the same way as the getitem ProcScript statement, but can also be used as part of an expression. For more information, see Lists and Sublists . Related Links List Handling in ProcScript Uniface provides ProcScript instructions that enable you to build lists, retrieve items from lists, and sort lists based on various criteria. $idpart Return the ID part of an associative list item. $valuepart Return the value part of an associative list item.
